name: Triglav
description: Triglav is a prominent deity in Slavic mythology, particularly venerated in the areas of Szczecin and Volgost. He is typically depicted as having three heads, representing dominion over the sky, earth, and underworld. Some interpretations suggest the three heads symbolize past, present, and future, or the three realms of heaven, earth, and the netherworld. Triglav is often associated with war, victory, and prophecy. Legends say his priests covered his face with a golden cloth because he saw too much sin. He was considered a protector, a guardian of the land, and a provider of justice. Offerings were frequently made to him to ensure success in battle and bountiful harvests. While the exact nature of his worship varied across different Slavic tribes, Triglav consistently held a position of considerable importance. He embodies the power and complexity of the Slavic pantheon, reflecting the interconnectedness of the cosmic realms and the human experience. The etymology of his name, meaning 'three-headed,' directly reflects his most distinctive feature and reinforces his triune nature. He represents a powerful force, embodying strength, foresight, and the interconnectedness of all things. Despite the suppression of Slavic paganism, the memory of Triglav endures, a testament to the enduring power of ancient beliefs and the rich cultural heritage of the Slavic people. Statues and idols were erected in his honor, and festivals were held to celebrate his power and influence. His influence can still be seen in some modern-day symbols and traditions, subtly echoing the beliefs of a bygone era. Triglav remains a fascinating figure, a symbol of Slavic identity and the enduring power of mythology.
subtitle: Three-headed god of sky, earth, underworld

keyMyths.title: The Golden Cloth
keyMyths.description: Priests covered Triglav's face with a golden cloth, believing he saw too much sin and wickedness in the world, shielding him from the sight.

keyMyths.title: Triune Dominion
keyMyths.description: Triglav's three heads represent his dominion over the sky, earth, and underworld, making him a powerful ruler of all realms.

keyMyths.title: Prophecies of War
keyMyths.description: Triglav was consulted for prophecies, particularly regarding the outcomes of wars and battles, his wisdom sought by leaders and warriors alike.

sources.author: Helmold
sources.description: Medieval chronicle providing early accounts of Slavic beliefs.
sources.text: Medieval chronicle providing early accounts of Slavic beliefs.

sources.author: Herbord
